hero.reviews: +100 garages transformed — Montreal, South Shore & Laval
hero.title: Your garage
hero.subtitle: We install a professional polyaspartic floor in just one day. Result: a showroom-worthy finish — durable for 15 years, slip-resistant, resistant to salt and oil.
hero.priceStorage: Single garage with storage (300-450 sq ft)

problem.title: Your garage floor
problem.subtitle: Aging, untreated concrete is a problem that gets worse with every Quebec season. The good news? There's a permanent solution.
problem.problem1Desc: Porous concrete absorbs everything. The more you scrub, the worse it gets.
problem.problem2Title: Salt corrodes and deteriorates in winter
problem.problem2Desc: Every winter, calcium slowly eats away at your floor.
problem.problem3Desc: Water seeps in, freezes, and progressively breaks apart the concrete.
problem.problem4Title: Floor that dusts and dirties everything
problem.problem4Desc: Concrete dust settles on your wheels, tools, and car.
problem.problem5Desc: A gray, stained floor doesn't represent your home's value.
problem.problem6Desc: You clean it, it gets dirty again immediately. Unsealed concrete is unmanageable.
problem.bridgeText: Polyaspartic coating is the same technology used in car dealerships, commercial warehouses, and industrial garages. We bring it to your home.

beforeAfter.title: Slide to see
beforeAfter.subtitle: Every photo is taken at a real Garage Express customer's home in Montreal, on the South Shore or in Laval. Slide the cursor to see the transformation.

benefits.title: This isn't epoxy.
benefits.subtitle: Polyaspartic is what professionals use when epoxy isn't enough. Cures 5× faster, superior resistance, longer lifespan.
benefits.benefit1Desc: Our technicians arrive in the morning and leave in the evening. The floor is ready to use 24h after application. Zero prolonged inconvenience.
benefits.benefit2Desc: Not just materials. Our warranty covers all the work. If anything happens within 15 years, we come back. Period.
benefits.benefit3Title: Resistant to everything a Quebec winter can throw at it
benefits.benefit3Desc: De-icing salt, calcium, motor oil, antifreeze. Polyaspartic doesn't absorb — it repels everything. A damp mop and it's clean.
benefits.benefit4Desc: Concrete expands and contracts. Our coating is flexible and follows the movement — without cracking or peeling, season after season.
benefits.benefit5Title: Twice as hard as traditional epoxy
benefits.benefit5Desc: Polyaspartic hardens faster and achieves superior resistance to epoxy. Ideal for garages with heavy vehicles, ATVs, or equipment.
benefits.benefit6Desc: Decorative flakes, custom colors, matte or glossy finish. Your garage can look like what you see in car dealerships.

process.title: What happens
process.subtitle: We don't believe in black boxes. Here's exactly what our technicians do, step by step, from arrival to departure.
process.step1Desc: We start early. Our technicians grind the concrete with a professional grinder to create the right adhesion texture. Dust is captured by a HEPA vacuum — no mess.
process.step1Detail: This is the most critical step. Poor preparation is the #1 cause of floors that peel. We never cut corners.
process.step2Desc: We inject high-strength polymer into every crack, pore, and irregularity. We let it cure, then grind level. Result: a perfectly flat surface.
process.step2Detail: Minor small cracks are included in the price. Major structural cracks are assessed on-site.
process.step3Desc: Application of the high-adhesion primer. The polyaspartic penetrates the concrete and creates a near-permanent chemical bond — far stronger than standard epoxy.
process.step3Detail: Express cure time: only 45 minutes, vs. 8–24h for epoxy. That's why we can do everything in 1 day.
process.step4Desc: Color flakes are spread by hand across the entire surface. You chose your color in advance. Uniform density, no bare spots, no overloaded areas.
process.step4Detail: 137 Torginol colors available. You choose your color in advance. The finish looks like natural granite.
process.step5Desc: The final protective layer is applied in two passes. It provides the gloss, slip resistance, and stain resistance. This is your 15-year armor.
process.step5Detail: UV-stable — won't yellow like epoxy. Chemical resistant. Mop-cleanable without special products.
process.step6Desc: We inspect every square foot with you. We pack up our equipment, clean the perimeter. Your garage is left as we found it — except for the floor.
process.step6Detail: You can walk on it the same evening. Vehicles can enter after 24h. Full strength reached in 72h.
process.endBadge: 5:00 PM — We leave. You love it.
process.endText: Complete installation in ~8h. Ready to walk on same evening. Vehicles in after 24h. Full strength in 72h.

testimonials.title: What your
testimonials.subtitle: These reviews are from real customers in Montreal, on the South Shore and in Laval. No fake testimonials, no fake stars.
testimonials.review1Title: Exactly what was promised. To the letter.
testimonials.review1Text: I was worried about hidden fees — it didn't happen. The quote price is exactly what I paid. The team arrived at 8am and left at 5pm. The next morning, I brought in my F-150 and ATV. The floor is impeccable. I recommend them without hesitation.
testimonials.review2Title: My husband thought it was too expensive. He changed his mind.
testimonials.review2Text: We were torn between cheap epoxy from Home Depot and Garage Express. My husband thought $2,750 was a lot. Now that we see the result, we only regret not doing it sooner. The floor is beautiful and so easy to clean. December salt — one mop and it's gone.
testimonials.review3Text: Third time dealing with a garage coating company. The first two times were disasters (peeling, cracks after 2 winters). Garage Express is a different league. The team explains every step, the installation is clean, and it's been 14 months now — no problems. This is what good work looks like.
testimonials.review4Title: My garage is prettier than my living room now.
testimonials.review4Text: I'm joking, but it's almost true. The finish is glossy, the gray flakes give a really luxurious look. My neighbors have asked for your contact twice already. The one-day installation was a big plus — no second day to clear in my schedule.
testimonials.review5Title: Responsive, punctual, clean. What more could you ask for?
testimonials.review5Text: I called on a Friday, got a response Saturday morning, and installation was done the following Wednesday. The team protected my walls with plastic, vacuumed dust as they went, and left the garage impeccable when they left. Plus the floor of course. Well-deserved 5-star service.
testimonials.review6Text: My real estate agent told me polyaspartic flooring adds perceived value when selling. But beyond that, I now love my garage. I do yoga in there in the summer. I'm laughing, but it's true. No more dust, no more oil stains, no more powdering concrete.

about.title: A local team.
about.p1Pre: Garage Express is a team of certified technicians based on the South Shore. We've been doing this for years, and we've learned one thing:
about.p1Bold: homeowners just want it done right, at the quoted price, without hassle.
about.p2: We exclusively use commercial-grade polyaspartic — the same technology found in car dealership garages, industrial warehouses, and medical clinics. Not big-box hardware store products.
about.p3Post: . We intentionally limit our volume so every client gets the level of care they deserve. That's why we have a 5/5 rating on Google.
about.card1Desc: We know the winter conditions here. We install for them.
about.card2Desc: Ongoing training, commercial-grade products, high standards.
about.card3Desc: Your neighbors know us. Ask them.
about.card4Desc: Diamond grinders, HEPA vacuums, precision mixing systems.

faq.title: Frequently asked
faq.subtitle: We answer the real questions people hesitate to ask.
faq.callQuestion: Have a question that's not here?
faq.q1: Is it really installed in 1 day?
faq.a1: Yes — without exception. Our technicians arrive around 8am and leave late afternoon. That same day, you can walk on the floor. After 24h, vehicles can enter. That's the main advantage of polyaspartic over epoxy: it cures 5× faster.
faq.q2: What's the difference between polyaspartic and epoxy?
faq.a2: Polyaspartic is a technological evolution of epoxy. It cures faster, resists UV better (doesn't yellow), is more flexible (handles Quebec freeze-thaw cycles), and lasts longer. Epoxy is still a good product, but polyaspartic is what professionals choose today for high-performance applications.
faq.q3: Does the price really include everything? Will there be surprises?
faq.a3: The price includes: surface preparation (grinding), minor crack repairs, base coat, decorative flakes, top coat, and cleanup. The only possible exception: major structural cracks (wider than 5mm or deep). We warn you in advance if that's the case after visual inspection.
faq.q4: Do I need to completely empty my garage?
faq.a4: Ideally yes — it speeds up installation and ensures complete coverage. If you can't move everything, our technicians can work in sections and move items during installation (depending on volume). Contact us to discuss beforehand.
faq.q5: Does it really hold up to Quebec winters?
faq.a5: Absolutely. It's designed for that. Polyaspartic is flexible, so it follows the concrete's expansion and contraction with freeze-thaw cycles without peeling or cracking. It's also resistant to de-icing salt, calcium, and sudden temperature changes from -30°C to +40°C.
faq.q6: How long is the warranty and what does it cover?
faq.a6: The warranty is 15 years and covers both materials AND labor. If the coating peels, cracks, chips, or shows defects unrelated to impact or abnormal wear, we come back to repair it at no cost. It's a real warranty, not just marketing.
faq.q7: Can I choose the flake color?
faq.a7: Yes. You choose from 137 Torginol colors available directly on our website. Custom blends outside the catalog are available for an additional $200.
faq.q8: Why not just buy an epoxy kit from Home Depot?
faq.a8: You can. But here's what typically happens: the product is low residential grade, surface preparation is inadequate, and the floor starts peeling after 1-2 winters. You do it again. In total, you've spent more than our package price, had the hassle of doing it yourself twice, and the result is inferior. Our 15-year warranty saves you all that.
faq.q9: My concrete is damp — is that a problem?
faq.a9: Not necessarily. Our primer is designed to resist residual moisture up to 8 lbs/1000 sq ft. Before any installation, our technicians measure moisture levels with a Tramex meter. If levels are too high, we tell you clearly and reschedule — we never install on a surface that will compromise long-term adhesion.
faq.q10: When can I park my car?
faq.a10: Exact timeline after installation: walk on it → same evening. Park a vehicle → after 48 hours. First wash → wait 7 days. Full chemical resistance → 2 weeks. Polyaspartic cures 5× faster than epoxy — that's why we can complete everything in 1 day.
faq.q11: Why does your product cost more than a hardware store kit?
faq.a11: A hardware store epoxy kit = 1 thin residential-grade coat with no professional prep. Our system = 4 layers (primer + base coat + flakes + top coat) with mechanical shot blasting. Total thickness: 28–35 mils vs 3–4 mils for a DIY kit. Our floor lasts 15+ years, a DIY kit lasts 1–2 winters. Over 15 years, our price is lower — and you never redo it.
faq.q12: My concrete is 30–40 years old. Can it still be coated?
faq.a12: In the vast majority of cases, yes. Concrete age isn't the determining factor — it's structural condition. Our technicians assess the slab during the visit: as long as the concrete is solid and not delaminating, shot blasting creates the necessary adhesion profile. Minor cracks are repaired and included in the price.
faq.q13: Will the floor yellow over time?
faq.a13: No. This is the key advantage of aliphatic polyaspartic. Our top coat is formulated for maximum UV stability — tested at ΔE < 2.0 after 500 hours of UV exposure (ASTM 3424). Even near a window or in semi-outdoor use, the color stays stable. Epoxy yellows because it's aromatic. Aliphatic polyaspartic doesn't — by chemical design.
faq.q14: How do I maintain my floor?
faq.a14: Very simple. First week: don't wash. Regular upkeep: damp mop with mild soap. Oil or calcium stains: damp cloth, they come right off. Avoid: extreme pressure washing, very hot water (max 49°C / 120°F), abrasive products. Cleaning every 2–4 weeks depending on use is plenty. This is one of the reasons our clients love this floor.

finalCta.title: Your garage
finalCta.subtitle: Every season you wait is another winter the salt and calcium do their work. One day of installation. 15 years of results.

footer.madewith: Made with ❤ on the South Shore

systemLayers.title: The
systemLayers.subtitle: Not paint. Not a kit. A professional system at 28 to 35 mils thick — the same technology used in industrial garages and car dealerships.
systemLayers.layer1Desc: Penetrates the concrete and creates a chemical bond. Resistant to residual moisture up to 8 lbs/1000 sq ft. Cures in 1 hour — allowing same-day continuation.
systemLayers.layer2Desc: The thickest layer in the system. High solids, superior abrasion resistance. Cures in 2 hours — that's why the full system can be installed in 1 day.
systemLayers.layer3Desc: Hand-broadcast immediately into the wet base coat. Deliver the granite or terrazzo look, add slip resistance, and visually protect the surface.
systemLayers.layer4Desc: The final protection layer. Maximum UV resistance (never yellows), superior chemical resistance, long-lasting gloss. This layer carries the 15-year warranty.

returnToService.title: When can you
returnToService.step1Desc: Polyaspartic cures in a few hours. No need to wait 3 days like with epoxy.
returnToService.step2Desc: After 48h, the surface is ready for the weight and heat of your tires.
returnToService.step3Desc: Wait 7 days before the first water cleaning for optimal cure.
returnToService.step4Desc: Maximum resistance to chemicals, motor oil, salt, and calcium.
